documentation.file_docstring: Best-effort process-tree termination with graceful shutdown. - Windows: use taskkill /T to include descendants. Sends SIGTERM-equivalent first (without /F), then force-kills if process survives. - Unix: send SIGTERM to process group first, wait grace period, then SIGKILL. This gives child processes a chance to clean up (close connections, remove temp files, terminate their own children) before being hard-killed.
inline_comments: Step 1: Try graceful SIGTERM to process group
Process group doesn't exist or we lack permission - try direct
Step 2: Wait grace period, then SIGKILL if still alive
Fall through to direct pid kill
Process exited between liveness check and kill
Step 2: Wait grace period, then force kill only if pid still exists.
This avoids unconditional delayed /F kills after graceful shutdown.

functions.name: killProcessTree
functions.doc: Best-effort process-tree termination with graceful shutdown.   - Windows: use taskkill  to include descendants. Sends SI
